articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision 2047: Green Growth & Digital India' Blueprint
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched a comprehensive blueprint for 'Vision 2047: Green Growth & Digital India', outlining key strategies for sustainable development and technological advancement.
• The plan emphasizes a multi-pronged approach focusing on renewable energy expansion, circular economy principles, and digital public infrastructure.
• Key targets include achieving significant reductions in carbon emissions, boosting green manufacturing capabilities, and ensuring universal digital literacy and access.
• The blueprint integrates economic growth with environmental sustainability, aiming to position India as a global leader in green technology and digital innovation.
• It calls for increased private sector investment, international collaborations, and a robust regulatory framework to support the envisioned transformations.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly meeting, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was largely anticipated, reflecting the MPC's cautious approach towards managing persistent retail inflation, particularly in food and core sectors.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and domestic supply-side challenges as key factors influencing the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting economic growth.
• Future policy actions will remain data-dependent, with a close watch on monsoon performance and global commodity prices.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Rigour in Environmental Impact Assessment Processes
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today delivered a landmark judgment, emphasizing the need for stricter adherence to Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) norms for infrastructure projects.
• The ruling mandates greater public participation, transparency, and independent expert scrutiny in the EIA process to prevent environmental degradation.
• It specifically highlighted concerns regarding post-facto environmental clearances and stressed the importance of cumulative impact assessments for projects in ecologically sensitive areas.
• The Court directed the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) to review and strengthen existing guidelines to align with the principles of sustainable development.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for project developers and regulatory bodies, promoting more responsible environmental governance.

articles.trivia: The Environment Protection Act of 1986 provides the legal framework for Environmental Impact Assessment in India.

articles.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Show Promising Progress on Key Chapters
articles.explainer:
• The latest round of negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) concluded with positive indications of progress on several key chapters.
• Both sides reported significant headway in areas such as trade in goods, services, and investment protection, moving closer to a comprehensive agreement.
• Points of contention, including market access for agricultural products and intellectual property rights, are still being deliberated but with a constructive approach.
• A joint statement highlighted the shared commitment to concluding the FTA swiftly, recognizing its potential to boost bilateral trade and economic cooperation.
• The agreement is expected to unlock substantial opportunities for Indian industries, particularly in textiles, automotive components, and IT services, while attracting European investments.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Remote Sensing Satellite for Enhanced Earth Observation
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a next-generation remote sensing satellite aboard the PSLV-C58 from Sriharikota.
• The satellite, equipped with advanced hyperspectral imaging sensors, will provide high-resolution data for applications in agriculture, urban planning, disaster management, and environmental monitoring.
• This mission marks a significant step in bolstering India's indigenous capabilities in earth observation and data analytics.
• The data generated will be crucial for precise resource mapping, climate change studies, and enhancing national security through improved surveillance.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the satellite's role in supporting various government initiatives, including 'Digital India' and 'Smart Cities' projects, by providing actionable geospatial intelligence.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Reviews National Education Policy Implementation, Highlights Key Challenges
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education presented its review report on the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report acknowledged significant strides in curriculum reform and digital education initiatives but pointed out persistent challenges in teacher training and infrastructure development.
• Concerns were raised regarding the uneven pace of implementation across states and the need for greater financial allocation to achieve NEP's ambitious goals.
• The panel recommended targeted interventions for vocational education integration and early childhood care and education (ECCE) expansion.
• Emphasis was placed on ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially for marginalized sections, and addressing the digital divide in learning.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Maritime Security with New Coastal Surveillance Technologies in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• India today announced the deployment of advanced coastal surveillance systems and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to enhance its maritime domain awareness in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to secure its vast coastline, protect economic interests, and counter illicit activities such as piracy, smuggling, and illegal fishing.
• The new technologies include integrated radar networks, automated identification systems, and real-time data fusion centers.
• Defence Minister emphasized the importance of a 'net security provider' role for India in the IOR, fostering regional cooperation and stability.
• The move aligns with India's 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision, aiming for a peaceful and prosperous Indian Ocean.

articles.headline: India's SDG Progress Report Highlights Gains in Poverty Reduction, Gaps in Environmental Goals
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released its annual report on India's progress towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), showcasing mixed results.
• The report lauded significant advancements in poverty eradication (SDG 1), health and well-being (SDG 3), and clean energy access (SDG 7).
• However, it flagged persistent challenges in environmental sustainability goals, particularly those related to clean water and sanitation (SDG 6), life on land (SDG 15), and climate action (SDG 13).
• Data indicated disparities in state-wise performance, emphasizing the need for targeted interventions in lagging regions.
• The report called for greater investment in sustainable infrastructure, behavioral changes, and enhanced multi-stakeholder partnerships to accelerate SDG achievement by 2030.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Initiates Consultations on Uniform Civil Code
articles.explainer:
• A newly constituted parliamentary committee has commenced broad-based consultations on the feasibility and implications of implementing a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) across India.
• The committee is engaging with legal experts, religious leaders, civil society organizations, and various community representatives to gather diverse perspectives.
• Discussions are focusing on the constitutional provisions, historical background, and potential socio-cultural impact of a UCC on personal laws.
• The move reignites a long-standing debate in India about balancing individual rights with religious freedom and national integration.
• The committee aims to present a comprehensive report to Parliament, outlining a roadmap for potential reforms in personal laws.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Deep Tech Startups and Innovation Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a new 'Deep Tech Startup Policy' aimed at fostering innovation in cutting-edge technologies.
• The policy offers a comprehensive framework including enhanced funding mechanisms, R&D grants, intellectual property protection, and market access support for deep tech ventures.
• It seeks to position India as a global hub for innovation in areas like Artificial Intelligence, Quantum Computing, Biotechnology, and Advanced Materials.
• The initiative emphasizes collaboration between academia, industry, and government to create a robust ecosystem for translating research into commercially viable products.
• This move is expected to attract significant investment, create high-skilled jobs, and drive India's technological self-reliance.

articles.headline: India Actively Engages in Indo-Pacific Economic Framework, Focus on Supply Chain Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India continues its active participation in the Indo-Pacific Economic Framework for Prosperity (IPEF), with a strong focus on strengthening supply chain resilience.
• The latest ministerial meeting saw India advocating for robust frameworks to prevent disruptions and ensure stability in critical sectors like semiconductors and pharmaceuticals.
• Discussions also covered pillars of trade, clean energy, and decarbonization, with India emphasizing equitable and inclusive economic growth in the region.
• IPEF, a US-led initiative, aims to enhance economic cooperation among member countries by setting high standards in various trade-related areas.
• India's engagement is crucial for diversifying its trade partnerships and integrating further into global value chains, especially in the context of geopolitical shifts.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Increasing Vulnerability of Indian Agriculture to Climate Change
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) has warned of the increasing vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate change impacts.
• The study projects significant yield losses for major crops like wheat, rice, and maize due to rising temperatures, erratic rainfall, and extreme weather events.
• It calls for accelerated adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices, including drought-resistant varieties, precision farming, and improved irrigation techniques.
• The report also emphasizes the need for robust early warning systems and crop insurance schemes to protect farmers from climate-induced losses.
• Policy recommendations include greater investment in agricultural research and development, diversification of cropping patterns, and promotion of sustainable land management practices.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Indus Valley Civilization Site in Gujarat
articles.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists from the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a previously unknown Indus Valley Civilization (IVC) site in Gujarat.
• The preliminary excavations have revealed significant artifacts, including pottery, seals, and structural remains, suggesting a well-established settlement.
• The site is believed to be a contemporary of major Harappan cities and could offer new insights into the trade networks and cultural practices of the IVC.
• Researchers are particularly interested in understanding the site's role in maritime trade, given Gujarat's historical coastal connections.
• This discovery adds to the growing number of IVC sites, especially in the western parts of India, broadening our understanding of the civilization's geographical spread and diversity.

articles.trivia: Dholavira, also in Gujarat, is a major Harappan city and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its unique water management system.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Achieves Key Milestones in Quantum Computing and Communication
articles.explainer:
• The National Quantum Mission (NQM) announced significant progress in its research and development efforts across quantum computing, communication, and sensing technologies.
• Scientists have successfully demonstrated entanglement-based quantum communication over longer distances and achieved breakthroughs in building multi-qubit quantum processors.
• The mission aims to make India a global leader in quantum technologies, with applications ranging from secure communication to advanced drug discovery and materials science.
• Collaborative projects involving premier research institutions, universities, and industry partners are accelerating the pace of innovation.
• This progress underscores India's commitment to strategic technologies and its potential to contribute to the next generation of technological advancements.

articles.headline: Global Supply Chain Shifts: Opportunities and Challenges for Indian Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) explores the impact of ongoing global supply chain reconfigurations on India's manufacturing sector.
• The report identifies significant opportunities for India to attract foreign investment and increase its share in global manufacturing, driven by 'China Plus One' strategies.
• However, it also highlights challenges such as infrastructure bottlenecks, skill gaps, and the need for greater integration into global value chains.
• Recommendations include targeted policy support for key manufacturing sectors, skill development programs, and investment in logistics and port infrastructure.
• The shifts present a critical juncture for India to bolster its 'Make in India' initiative and enhance its competitiveness in a reshaped global economy.

articles.headline: National Mental Health Awareness Campaign Launched to Combat Stigma and Improve Access to Care
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with various NGOs, today launched a nationwide mental health awareness campaign.
• The initiative aims to reduce the stigma associated with mental illness, promote early identification of symptoms, and encourage seeking professional help.
• Key components include public service announcements, community outreach programs, and digital platforms offering resources and tele-counseling services.
• The campaign also focuses on training frontline health workers to provide basic mental health support and referrals in rural areas.
• This comprehensive effort is part of India's commitment to strengthening its mental healthcare infrastructure and ensuring universal access to support services.

articles.headline: Government Revises E-Waste Management Policy for Enhanced Circularity and Producer Responsibility
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has announced a revision of the E-Waste Management Rules, aiming for a more circular economy approach.
• The updated policy introduces stricter targets for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and mandates the use of recycled content in new electronic products.
• It streamlines the collection, dismantling, and recycling processes, with an emphasis on formalizing the informal e-waste sector and ensuring safe disposal practices.
• The revisions also include provisions for greater public awareness and consumer participation in responsible e-waste disposal.
• This move is critical for mitigating environmental pollution, conserving valuable resources, and promoting sustainable consumption and production patterns in India.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Discuss Enhanced Connectivity Projects for Regional Integration
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held high-level discussions focusing on enhancing bilateral connectivity projects, including ferry services, power grid interconnections, and energy pipelines.
• The talks aimed at strengthening economic ties and promoting regional integration within the South Asian context.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of improving people-to-people contact and facilitating trade through improved infrastructure.
• Discussions also touched upon potential for joint development of port infrastructure and renewable energy projects.
• This collaboration is seen as crucial for Sri Lanka's economic recovery and for India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy, fostering stability and prosperity in the region.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Stronger Oversight for Digital Personal Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary committee tasked with reviewing the implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, submitted its interim report.
• The panel recommended stronger parliamentary oversight mechanisms for the Data Protection Board and emphasized the need for periodic reviews of the Act's effectiveness.
• Concerns were raised regarding the adequacy of enforcement resources and the need for greater public awareness about data privacy rights.
• The report also suggested clarity on the definition of 'significant data fiduciaries' and their enhanced compliance obligations.
• This recommendation aims to ensure robust protection of citizens' digital rights while fostering a thriving digital economy.

articles.headline: National Mission on Natural Farming Shows Promising Results in Enhancing Soil Health and Farmer Incomes
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released an assessment report on the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF), highlighting positive outcomes.
• The report indicated significant improvements in soil organic carbon content, water retention capacity, and biodiversity in NMNF-adopted farms.
• Farmers practicing natural farming reported reduced input costs, leading to enhanced net incomes, despite initial challenges in yield transition.
• The mission promotes chemical-free agriculture based on traditional Indian practices, focusing on cow-based farming and on-farm input generation.
• The government plans to expand the mission's reach, offering more training and financial incentives to encourage wider adoption of natural farming techniques.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Vision 2047: Green Growth & Digital India' blueprint, consider the following statements:
1. It primarily focuses on achieving universal digital literacy and access by 2047.
2. It emphasizes a circular economy model and green manufacturing capabilities.
3. The blueprint aims to completely phase out fossil fuels by 2047.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is partially correct but not the primary focus; the blueprint is comprehensive. Statement 3 is incorrect; while it focuses on renewable energy, a complete phase-out by 2047 is an ambitious and likely unrealistic goal not explicitly stated. Statement 2 is correct, as the blueprint explicitly emphasizes circular economy principles and boosting green manufacturing capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India is a statutory body constituted under the RBI Act, 1934, responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in India:
1. The Environment Protection Act of 1986 provides the legal framework for EIA.
2. The recent Supreme Court ruling mandates greater public participation and independent expert scrutiny in the EIA process.
3. Post-facto environmental clearances are explicitly encouraged under the latest guidelines.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is correct as per the news. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Supreme Court ruling specifically highlighted concerns regarding and implicitly discouraged post-facto environmental clearances, stressing rigorous adherence to norms *before* project implementation.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India, which Article of the Constitution falls under the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) and mentions the State's endeavour to secure a UCC?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution, enshrined in Part IV (Directive Principles of State Policy), states: 'The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.'

prelimsMocks.question: The 'China Plus One' strategy, often discussed in the context of global supply chain shifts, primarily refers to:
options: (a) China's strategy to expand its economic influence to one additional country.
(b) A diplomatic initiative to include China in one more international trade bloc.
(c) Companies diversifying their manufacturing and supply chain operations beyond China to other countries.
(d) China's policy to increase its domestic consumption by at least one percentage point annually.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Companies diversifying their manufacturing and supply chain operations beyond China to other countries.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'China Plus One' strategy is a business strategy in which companies diversify their manufacturing and supply chain operations by adding another country to their existing operations in China, reducing reliance on a single market.

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Indus Valley Civilization Site in Gujarat
GS1.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists from the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a previously unknown Indus Valley Civilization (IVC) site in Gujarat.
• The preliminary excavations have revealed significant artifacts, including pottery, seals, and structural remains, suggesting a well-established settlement.
• The site is believed to be a contemporary of major Harappan cities and could offer new insights into the trade networks and cultural practices of the IVC.
• Researchers are particularly interested in understanding the site's role in maritime trade, given Gujarat's historical coastal connections.
• This discovery adds to the growing number of IVC sites, especially in the western parts of India, broadening our understanding of the civilization's geographical spread and diversity.

GS1.trivia: Dholavira, also in Gujarat, is a major Harappan city and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its unique water management system.

GS2.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Show Promising Progress on Key Chapters
GS2.explainer:

• Both sides reported significant headway in areas such as trade in goods, services, and investment protection, moving closer to a comprehensive agreement.
• Points of contention, including market access for agricultural products and intellectual property rights, are still being deliberated but with a constructive approach.
• A joint statement highlighted the shared commitment to concluding the FTA swiftly, recognizing its potential to boost bilateral trade and economic cooperation.
• The agreement is expected to unlock substantial opportunities for Indian industries, particularly in textiles, automotive components, and IT services, while attracting European investments.

GS2.explainer:

• The report acknowledged significant strides in curriculum reform and digital education initiatives but pointed out persistent challenges in teacher training and infrastructure development.
• Concerns were raised regarding the uneven pace of implementation across states and the need for greater financial allocation to achieve NEP's ambitious goals.
• The panel recommended targeted interventions for vocational education integration and early childhood care and education (ECCE) expansion.
• Emphasis was placed on ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially for marginalized sections, and addressing the digital divide in learning.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Initiates Consultations on Uniform Civil Code
GS2.explainer:
• A newly constituted parliamentary committee has commenced broad-based consultations on the feasibility and implications of implementing a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) across India.
• The committee is engaging with legal experts, religious leaders, civil society organizations, and various community representatives to gather diverse perspectives.
• Discussions are focusing on the constitutional provisions, historical background, and potential socio-cultural impact of a UCC on personal laws.
• The move reignites a long-standing debate in India about balancing individual rights with religious freedom and national integration.
• The committee aims to present a comprehensive report to Parliament, outlining a roadmap for potential reforms in personal laws.

• IPEF, a US-led initiative, aims to enhance economic cooperation among member countries by setting high standards in various trade-related areas.

GS3.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly meeting, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was largely anticipated, reflecting the MPC's cautious approach towards managing persistent retail inflation, particularly in food and core sectors.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and domestic supply-side challenges as key factors influencing the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting economic growth.
• Future policy actions will remain data-dependent, with a close watch on monsoon performance and global commodity prices.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Rigour in Environmental Impact Assessment Processes
GS3.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today delivered a landmark judgment, emphasizing the need for stricter adherence to Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) norms for infrastructure projects.
• The ruling mandates greater public participation, transparency, and independent expert scrutiny in the EIA process to prevent environmental degradation.
• It specifically highlighted concerns regarding post-facto environmental clearances and stressed the importance of cumulative impact assessments for projects in ecologically sensitive areas.
• The Court directed the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) to review and strengthen existing guidelines to align with the principles of sustainable development.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for project developers and regulatory bodies, promoting more responsible environmental governance.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Remote Sensing Satellite for Enhanced Earth Observation
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a next-generation remote sensing satellite aboard the PSLV-C58 from Sriharikota.
• The satellite, equipped with advanced hyperspectral imaging sensors, will provide high-resolution data for applications in agriculture, urban planning, disaster management, and environmental monitoring.
• This mission marks a significant step in bolstering India's indigenous capabilities in earth observation and data analytics.
• The data generated will be crucial for precise resource mapping, climate change studies, and enhancing national security through improved surveillance.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the satellite's role in supporting various government initiatives, including 'Digital India' and 'Smart Cities' projects, by providing actionable geospatial intelligence.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Maritime Security with New Coastal Surveillance Technologies in Indian Ocean Region
GS3.explainer:
• India today announced the deployment of advanced coastal surveillance systems and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to enhance its maritime domain awareness in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to secure its vast coastline, protect economic interests, and counter illicit activities such as piracy, smuggling, and illegal fishing.
• The new technologies include integrated radar networks, automated identification systems, and real-time data fusion centers.
• Defence Minister emphasized the importance of a 'net security provider' role for India in the IOR, fostering regional cooperation and stability.
• The move aligns with India's 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision, aiming for a peaceful and prosperous Indian Ocean.

GS3.headline: India's SDG Progress Report Highlights Gains in Poverty Reduction, Gaps in Environmental Goals
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released its annual report on India's progress towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), showcasing mixed results.
• The report lauded significant advancements in poverty eradication (SDG 1), health and well-being (SDG 3), and clean energy access (SDG 7).
• However, it flagged persistent challenges in environmental sustainability goals, particularly those related to clean water and sanitation (SDG 6), life on land (SDG 15), and climate action (SDG 13).
• Data indicated disparities in state-wise performance, emphasizing the need for targeted interventions in lagging regions.
• The report called for greater investment in sustainable infrastructure, behavioral changes, and enhanced multi-stakeholder partnerships to accelerate SDG achievement by 2030.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Deep Tech Startups and Innovation Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a new 'Deep Tech Startup Policy' aimed at fostering innovation in cutting-edge technologies.
• The policy offers a comprehensive framework including enhanced funding mechanisms, R&D grants, intellectual property protection, and market access support for deep tech ventures.
• It seeks to position India as a global hub for innovation in areas like Artificial Intelligence, Quantum Computing, Biotechnology, and Advanced Materials.
• The initiative emphasizes collaboration between academia, industry, and government to create a robust ecosystem for translating research into commercially viable products.
• This move is expected to attract significant investment, create high-skilled jobs, and drive India's technological self-reliance.

GS3.headline: Report Highlights Increasing Vulnerability of Indian Agriculture to Climate Change
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) has warned of the increasing vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate change impacts.
• The study projects significant yield losses for major crops like wheat, rice, and maize due to rising temperatures, erratic rainfall, and extreme weather events.
• It calls for accelerated adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices, including drought-resistant varieties, precision farming, and improved irrigation techniques.
• The report also emphasizes the need for robust early warning systems and crop insurance schemes to protect farmers from climate-induced losses.
• Policy recommendations include greater investment in agricultural research and development, diversification of cropping patterns, and promotion of sustainable land management practices.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Achieves Key Milestones in Quantum Computing and Communication
GS3.explainer:
• The National Quantum Mission (NQM) announced significant progress in its research and development efforts across quantum computing, communication, and sensing technologies.
• Scientists have successfully demonstrated entanglement-based quantum communication over longer distances and achieved breakthroughs in building multi-qubit quantum processors.
• The mission aims to make India a global leader in quantum technologies, with applications ranging from secure communication to advanced drug discovery and materials science.
• Collaborative projects involving premier research institutions, universities, and industry partners are accelerating the pace of innovation.
• This progress underscores India's commitment to strategic technologies and its potential to contribute to the next generation of technological advancements.

GS3.headline: Global Supply Chain Shifts: Opportunities and Challenges for Indian Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) explores the impact of ongoing global supply chain reconfigurations on India's manufacturing sector.
• The report identifies significant opportunities for India to attract foreign investment and increase its share in global manufacturing, driven by 'China Plus One' strategies.
• However, it also highlights challenges such as infrastructure bottlenecks, skill gaps, and the need for greater integration into global value chains.
• Recommendations include targeted policy support for key manufacturing sectors, skill development programs, and investment in logistics and port infrastructure.
• The shifts present a critical juncture for India to bolster its 'Make in India' initiative and enhance its competitiveness in a reshaped global economy.

GS3.headline: Government Revises E-Waste Management Policy for Enhanced Circularity and Producer Responsibility
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has announced a revision of the E-Waste Management Rules, aiming for a more circular economy approach.
• The updated policy introduces stricter targets for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and mandates the use of recycled content in new electronic products.
• It streamlines the collection, dismantling, and recycling processes, with an emphasis on formalizing the informal e-waste sector and ensuring safe disposal practices.
• The revisions also include provisions for greater public awareness and consumer participation in responsible e-waste disposal.
• This move is critical for mitigating environmental pollution, conserving valuable resources, and promoting sustainable consumption and production patterns in India.

GS3.headline: National Mission on Natural Farming Shows Promising Results in Enhancing Soil Health and Farmer Incomes
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released an assessment report on the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF), highlighting positive outcomes.
• The report indicated significant improvements in soil organic carbon content, water retention capacity, and biodiversity in NMNF-adopted farms.
• Farmers practicing natural farming reported reduced input costs, leading to enhanced net incomes, despite initial challenges in yield transition.
• The mission promotes chemical-free agriculture based on traditional Indian practices, focusing on cow-based farming and on-farm input generation.
• The government plans to expand the mission's reach, offering more training and financial incentives to encourage wider adoption of natural farming techniques.
